sentences.text: The RVI20 profiles document the initial set of unprivileged instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles

sentences.text: These provide a generic target for software toolchains and represent the minimum level of compatibility with RISC-V ratified standards.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles

sentences.text: The two profiles RVI20U32 and RVI20U64 correspond to the RV32I and RV64I base ISAs respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles

sentences.text: NOTE: These are designed as unprivileged profiles as opposed to user-mode profiles.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles

sentences.text: Code using this profile can run in any privilege mode, and so requested and fatal traps may be horizontal traps into an execution environment running in the same privilege mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles

sentences.text: RVI20U32 specifies the ISA features available to generic unprivileged execution environments.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile

sentences.text: RV32I is the mandatory base ISA for RVI20U32, and is little-endian.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: As per the unprivileged architecture specification, the `ecall` instruction causes a requested trap to the execution environment.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: Misaligned loads and stores might not be supported.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: The `fence.tso` instruction is mandatory.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: NOTE: The `fence.tso` instruction was incorrectly described as optional in the 2019 ratified specifications.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: Later versions of correctly indicate that the instruction is mandatory.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: There are no mandatory extensions for RVI20U32.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: extlink:m[] Integer multiplication and division.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: extlink:a[] Atomic instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Single-precision floating-point instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Double-precision floating-point instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The rationale to not include Q as an optional extension is that quad-precision floating-point is unlikely to be implemented in hardware, and so we do not require or expect software to expend effort optimizing use of Q instructions in case they are present.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: extlink:c[] Compressed Instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Instruction-fetch fence instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Misaligned loads and stores may be supported.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The Zicsr extension is not supported independent of the Zicntr or F extensions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Hardware performance counters.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U32 Profile > RVI20U32 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: RVI20U64 specifies the ISA features available to generic unprivileged execution environments.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile

sentences.text: RV64I is the mandatory base ISA for RVI20U64, and is little-endian.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: As per the unprivileged architecture specification, the `ecall` instruction causes a requested trap to the execution environment.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: Misaligned loads and stores might not be supported.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: The `fence.tso` instruction is mandatory.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: NOTE: The `fence.tso` instruction was incorrectly described as optional in the 2019 ratified specifications.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: However, `fence.tso` is encoded within the standard `fence` encoding such that implementations must treat it as a simple global fence if they do not natively support TSO-ordering optimizations.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: As software can always assume without any penalty that `fence.tso` is being exploited by a hardware implementation, there is no advantage to making the instruction a profile option.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: Later versions of the unprivileged ISA specifications correctly indicate that `fence.tso` is mandatory.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: There are no mandatory extensions for RVI20U64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: extlink:m[] Integer multiplication and division.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: extlink:a[] Atomic instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Single-precision floating-point instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Double-precision floating-point instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The rationale to not include Q as a profile option is that quad-precision floating-point is unlikely to be implemented in hardware, and so we do not require or expect software to expend effort optimizing use of Q instructions in case they are present.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: extlink:c[] Compressed Instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Instruction-fetch fence instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Misaligned loads and stores may be supported.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The Zicsr extension is not supported independent of the Zicntr or F extensions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Hardware performance counters.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVI20 Profiles > RVI20U64 Profile > RVI20U64 Optional Extensions
